behaviors.description: The content passed to summary-generation includes data from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure is at a module handoff boundary: three collectors plus report-assembly input to the summarizer. A unit test of only merge logic can miss wiring/omission bugs between source adapters and the assembly step. No real OS/network/LLM behavior is required to catch this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the data-collection and report-assembly modules together with controlled test doubles for the three source clients, then assert the exact payload sent to summary-generation contains fields from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log.

behaviors.description: If one data source is unavailable, report generation continues and marks that section as `data unavailable`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback behavior (source client failure -> assembler -> renderer), not just a single function algorithm. A pure unit test would likely miss exception-contract mismatches between modules. Real external infra is not strictly required if one source client is made to fail realistically at the boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute collection + assembly + rendering together while one source client returns timeout/error; verify the final report is produced, includes `data unavailable` for only that section, and process exit indicates success.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents key values in input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality, not only deterministic wiring. Mocking or stubbing summary-generation would hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. Because this is a single model-dependent step (not multi-agent sequence), Agentic is the minimum, not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative structured inputs with known key values to the real summary model and evaluate output against assertions/rubric (key numbers, trend direction, critical incidents). Run multiple trials to detect variance-related failures.

behaviors.description: Complete report flow (collection through email delivery) reaches configured recipient inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses real external boundaries (HTTP/internal APIs + email transport/inbox). Mocked delivery can pass while real SMTP/API auth, formatting, or deliverability fails. The failure boundary is end-to-end system behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in an environment with reachable test instances of the three data sources and a real test mailbox; verify one run delivers an email to configured recipients with expected subject/body and complete sections.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, report is saved locally and delivery is retried on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Requires real persistence and run-to-run behavior: failure during external delivery, local filesystem fallback artifact, then retry on a later execution. Mocking email/file/timing can hide real retry-state and I/O edge failures. Multi-step, but not multi-agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run 1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable; assert fallback file is written with report content and retry metadata. Execute run 2 after restoring email service; assert previously saved report is sent and marked delivered/cleared.
